introSection: Pepsi Orange Streak (formerly known as Pepsi Ripsaw) is an indoor steel roller coaster located at Nickelodeon Universe Mall of America in Bloomington, Minnesota, USA.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Pepsi Ripsaw was the only roller coaster which opened with the park (then called Knott's Camp Snoopy), on August 11, 1992.

For the 2008 season, the park reopened under its current name, Nickelodeon Universe. As part of the overhaul, Pepsi Ripsaw was renamed Pepsi Orange Streak and either the entirety of the trains or their outer shells were replaced.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: The ride has two tire propelled lifts. The track travels around the park and passes through two enclosed sections while intersecting with Log Chute, as well as the Fairly Odd Coaster. Pepsi Orange Streak finishes with a helix.

sections.title: Color scheme
sections.content: Dark green track with light green supports.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: Riders are held in by individual lap bars.

fullText:
Pepsi Orange Streak (formerly known as Pepsi Ripsaw) is an indoor steel roller coaster located at Nickelodeon Universe Mall of America in Bloomington, Minnesota, USA.

Pepsi Ripsaw was the only roller coaster which opened with the park (then called Knott's Camp Snoopy), on August 11, 1992.

For the 2008 season, the park reopened under its current name, Nickelodeon Universe. As part of the overhaul, Pepsi Ripsaw was renamed Pepsi Orange Streak and either the entirety of the trains or their outer shells were replaced.

The ride has two tire propelled lifts. The track travels around the park and passes through two enclosed sections while intersecting with Log Chute, as well as the Fairly Odd Coaster. Pepsi Orange Streak finishes with a helix.

Dark green track with light green supports.



Riders are held in by individual lap bars.
